general_feedback: The three @return descriptions added are accurate, concise, and consistent with the existing @param documentation. `updateWord` correctly conveys that storage may grow. `fromBitMask` correctly notes the bitset contains the bits represented by the array (defensive copy already covered in @param). `fromBitMaskNoCopy` correctly indicates the array backs the bitset (no copy). All follow Scaladoc conventions: start lowercase, use backticks for code references, single clear sentence.
